questions.q: An ideal gas is taken through a cycle of a process consisting of three stages: (i) isothermal expansion, (ii) adiabatic expansion, and (iii) isothermal compression. Which of the following statements is true regarding the overall change in internal energy of the gas?

A gas is compressed from state A to state B. If the process is carried out in a time of 1 sec, and another gas is compressed from state A to state B in a time of 10 sec, then which of the following will be the same for both the processes?

An ideal gas is enclosed in a cylinder fitted with a frictionless piston. The gas is compressed slowly and adiabatically from an initial volume of 2.5 L to a final volume of 1.25 L. Calculate the work done in the process.
questions.answer: The work done is given by W = 2P0(V1 - V2) / (γ - 1), where γ is the adiabatic index

questions.q: The average kinetic energy of the molecules of a gas is 4.2 × 10^(-21) J. What is the root mean square speed of the molecules?
True or False: An ideal gas has no volume.
An ideal gas is taken through a cycle of isothermal expansion and isothermal compression. What will be the net change in the internal energy of the gas?
A gas is compressed from a volume of 100 L to 50 L. If the work done in the process is 200 J, calculate the change in internal energy of the gas.
The average kinetic energy of the molecules of a gas is 2.5 × 10^(-21) J. What is the most probable speed of the molecules?
An ideal gas is taken through a cycle of adiabatic expansion and isothermal compression. What will be the net change in the internal energy of the gas?
A gas is expanded from a pressure of 2 × 10^5 Pa to 1 × 10^5 Pa. If the work done in the process is 100 J, calculate the change in internal energy of the gas.
